skill.name: bazel-build-optimization
skill.description: Optimize Bazel builds for large monorepos with practical configuration, caching, remote execution, and performance analysis guidance.
skill.summary: Improve Bazel monorepo build speed, caching, remote execution, and dependency analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network URLs, filesystem paths, weak crypto terms, and heuristic combinations. Context shows a Bazel markdown guide with legitimate examples, but the shell commands and remote cache settings can affect builds or upload artifacts if applied without review. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Bazel and shell commands require review before execution
medium_findings.description: The skill includes bash examples for bazel query, dependency graph generation, build profiling, and execution logging. These are legitimate Bazel operations, but an AI agent should not run them automatically because they can consume resources, write output files, or behave unexpectedly in large repositories.
medium_findings.recommendation: Require user confirmation before running commands, and scope commands to explicit targets where possible.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Actual shell command examples are present, including command substitution at line 305. The context is instructional Bazel usage, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Remote cache and execution settings can transmit build artifacts
medium_findings.description: The .bazelrc template includes remote cache, remote execution, and build event service endpoints with upload of local results enabled. The endpoints are placeholders, but applying similar settings to a real repository can send artifacts and build metadata to external services.
medium_findings.recommendation: Use trusted remote services, document data handling, and review which artifacts and metadata are uploaded before enabling these options.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network-related configuration is explicit and security relevant. The example uses placeholder domains, which lowers confidence that it indicates malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are dependency and documentation examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs in repository rule examples and resource links. The URLs point to Bazel, rules_python, and rules_js documentation or release artifacts, with no evidence of data exfiltration.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep dependency URLs pinned with verified hashes and update links during normal dependency maintenance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in Bazel configuration and documentation sections. No code path sends secrets or local data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Home cache paths are standard Bazel cache configuration
low_findings.description: The hidden home directory references configure Bazel disk and repository caches. They do not read credentials or target sensitive files, but users should ensure cache permissions match their environment.
low_findings.recommendation: Use project-approved cache locations and avoid shared cache directories that expose build artifacts to unintended users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines configure .cache paths for Bazel caching. This is a common local build pattern and not credential access.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and sensitive keyword detections are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and private key detections are caused by ordinary text or identifiers in the markdown guide. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, key material, seed phrases, or credential handling.
low_findings.recommendation: No security change is required for these keyword matches.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive markdown or Bazel rule fields, not cryptographic code or secrets. The static labels do not match the semantic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command substitution inside Bazel query example
dangerous_patterns.description: The query example embeds git diff output inside a Bazel query through shell command substitution. This can be fragile with unusual filenames and should be reviewed before use in automation.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Prefer a reviewed script or generated query file that safely handles filenames and empty change sets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is directly present at the cited line. It is a legitimate analysis pattern, but it has enough shell parsing risk to warrant a warning.

content.value_statement: Large Bazel repositories can suffer from slow builds, weak cache behavior, and unclear dependency graphs. This skill provides practical templates and review guidance for faster, more reliable Bazel workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Bazel for large monorepos and enterprise build systems.
Provides WORKSPACE and .bazelrc templates for dependencies, caching, remote execution, and CI settings.
Shows BUILD file patterns for TypeScript, Python, tests, binaries, and custom rules.
Provides Bazel query examples for dependency, reverse dependency, target, and graph analysis.
Suggests profiling commands for build speed, execution logs, and memory usage.
Summarizes Bazel best practices for caching, visibility, target size, and dependency pinning.
limitations: The skill is guidance-only and does not benchmark your repository automatically.
Examples use placeholders that must be adapted to your toolchains and remote services.
It does not verify third-party dependency versions or hashes for you.
Remote execution guidance requires existing infrastructure and security review.

use_cases.title: Plan a Bazel migration
use_cases.description: Map a monorepo into packages, targets, toolchains, and initial build files before migration work begins.

use_cases.title: Improve CI build performance
use_cases.description: Review cache, remote execution, and build metadata settings to reduce repeated work in continuous integration.

use_cases.title: Debug dependency growth
use_cases.description: Use query patterns to inspect dependency graphs, reverse dependencies, changed targets, and test target coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Review my Bazel setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Bazel workspace structure and suggest improvements for packages, BUILD files, and basic cache settings.

prompt_templates.title: Create build files for a library
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft Bazel BUILD file guidance for this TypeScript or Python library, including tests and public visibility choices.

prompt_templates.title: Tune CI caching
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my .bazelrc goals for CI and propose safe remote cache, local cache, and build event settings.

prompt_templates.title: Design remote execution strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Bazel remote execution plan for a large monorepo, including platforms, toolchains, security checks, and rollout steps.

output_examples.input: A team wants to speed up CI for a mixed TypeScript and Python monorepo.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a prioritized plan covering target structure, dependency pinning, cache settings, remote cache review, and profiling steps.

output_examples.input: A developer needs to understand why one library causes many rebuilds.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest dependency and reverse dependency analysis, graph review, target boundaries, and visibility improvements.

output_examples.input: A platform team is considering remote execution for Bazel.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline platform definitions, worker image requirements, artifact handling risks, and staged rollout checks.

best_practices: Pin external dependencies and verify hashes before using repository rules in production.
Start with narrow targets and clear visibility rules to improve cache reuse and ownership.
Review remote cache and execution settings with security and infrastructure owners before rollout.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder remote endpoints into production configuration.
Do not run broad Bazel commands across an entire monorepo without confirming scope and resource impact.
Do not rely on glob patterns for dependency declarations when explicit targets are available.

faq.question: Does this skill run Bazel commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides examples and guidance. Users should approve any command before execution.

faq.question: Can it configure remote caching for my company?
faq.answer: It can suggest configuration patterns, but your service endpoints, credentials, and data policies must be supplied by your team.

faq.question: Is it useful for small repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but the largest benefit is usually in monorepos or CI systems with repeated builds.

faq.question: Does it support both TypeScript and Python?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide includes example patterns for TypeScript and Python BUILD files.

faq.question: Can it find slow Bazel targets?
faq.answer: It can recommend profiling commands and analysis steps. It does not collect or interpret profiles without user-provided data.

faq.question: What should be reviewed before using remote execution?
faq.answer: Review worker images, artifact upload behavior, network endpoints, access control, and build metadata exposure.
